cross_category_overview: Across portfolios, regulators are converging on a more aggressive, cross-cutting enforcement posture — ASIC, APRA, ACCC, AUSTRAC, TGA, TEQSA, CASA and AMSA are simultaneously escalating supervisory intensity, litigation and licence actions, with AI/cyber resilience, AML/CTF tranche-two extension, and personnel/sovereign security emerging as shared compliance fault lines amplified by the 2026–27 Federal Budget. For KPMG advisory, this translates into concentrated demand for board-level AI and cyber governance uplift, AFSL/AML/export-control program remediation, and transition readiness across major structural reforms (NDIS, ATEC, payments modernisation, network pricing and approvals pathways), with compliance cost bases shifting materially onto tranche-two and defence-adjacent entities not previously in scope.

hub_wide.brief: Three regulator investment themes dominate the fortnight: ACCC's $67.7m enforcement uplift and AER's concurrent Victorian DNSP pricing trio plus Transgrid OT CPA signal a concentrated economic-regulation and OT-cyber spend; ASIC and AUSTRAC are simultaneously scaling cyber/AI supervision (26-092MR) and financial-integrity capability (new ML/PF updates, Financial Integrity Hub, VASP oversight) with live enforcement against Telstra Super, ABL Funds Management, Canva Group and Tabcorp; and NDIS eligibility reform plus ATEC stand-up and TEQSA's integrity push open material scheme-integrity and new-regulator build lanes. Clients squarely in the news this fortnight include Powercor, Jemena, United Energy, Transgrid, Telstra Super, Canva Group, Tabcorp, Veolia, Macquarie and ANU, alongside the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission and AHPRA as buyers of governance and workforce uplift. KPMG should concentrate BD effort on the AER Victorian pricing-and-OT cyber pursuit cluster, an ASIC/AUSTRAC cyber-and-AML board-uplift offer to AFSLs and RSEs, and an NDIS eligibility-decisioning and data-analytics play tied to the Albanese overhaul. Secondary plays sit in ATEC/TEQSA governance and AI-integrity assurance for Go8 providers, and a CASA Part 138/BVLOS plus AMSA modern-slavery readiness package for transport clients. Treat the Inspector-General of Aged Care resignation and ACQSC Bulletin #4 as a 48-hour partner-call trigger into aged-care provider boards. Defence primes facing first SAMS Act charges and export-controls Federal Court action need an integrated personnel-security and end-use assurance conversation now.

adam_key_messages.brief: This fortnight regulators have leaned harder into enforcement capability and market oversight — antitrust funding has been topped up, energy regulators have stepped up scrutiny of gas market performance and network pricing proposals, and environmental and planning settings are being recalibrated through the Budget, landfill levy litigation and faster renewable approvals — while the AI adoption and burden-reduction agendas continue to mature in the background. The opportunity that matters most for our advisory practice is helping regulators translate this enforcement and pricing intensity into defensible, data-driven decision-making, because fragmented systems and limited analytical depth remain the binding constraint on credible regulatory action. Convene our energy, competition and environmental regulatory leads in the next fortnight to shape a joint point of view on evidence-led enforcement and pricing oversight, and brief priority regulator clients on how we can stand up the data, AI governance and operating-model uplift required to deliver it.

ASIC's 26-092MR is a market-wide call for cyber uplift in the face of AI-accelerated threats — a direct mandate to Boards of regulated entities and a green light for assurance, AI risk and incident-readiness work.
